headline: Hungary celebrates 'return to Europe' after Orban's crushing electoral defeat
subhead: Péter Magyar's party, Tisza, won more than 53% of the vote in the legislative elections, far ahead of the prime minister's Fidesz, which managed barely 38%. Tisza is expected to secure 138 out of the 199 seats in Parliament, giving it a constitutional supermajority.

headline: 'Far from modernizing Hungary, Orban's economic policy has led the country into a dead end'
US military to begin naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz after failed talks over Iran war
In war-torn Lebanon, artists and cultural actors mobilize to support displaced families
subhead: Several spontaneous cultural initiatives, including in theaters, aim to help people driven from their homes by Israeli bombardments.

headline: 'They are asked to be both Madonna and whore': Photographer Philippa James captures her teenage daughter's daily life on social media
subhead: In her series 'Once a Slag…,' the British photographer explores the contradictions facing young people as they navigate identity, social pressures and the constant exposure of their lives online.

headline: French lawmakers to debate bill to return looted colonial-era art
subhead: France has been flooded with restitution demands, including from Algeria, Mali and Benin.

headline: In Algeria, the pope follows in the footsteps of the country's Christian history and reaches out to Muslims
subhead: On Monday, Leo XIV will become the first pontiff in history to visit Algeria, birthplace of the renowned Christian philosopher Saint Augustine. It was in Hippo, now Annaba, that the theologian wrote his most significant works in the fourth century.

Pope embarks on African tour, a strategic continent for Catholicism
subhead: From April 13 to 23, Pope Léon XIV is set to visit Algeria, Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea. The trip symbolizes the shift in the center of gravity of the Catholic Church, whose future now lies in Africa.

headline: 'Do you have any regrets about having to kill?' On Trans-Siberian Railway, Russian soldiers recount time in Ukraine
subhead: 'On Board the Trans-Siberian' (2/2). As the train speeds toward the Russian Far East, between Novosibirsk and Vladivostok, the brutality of the war in Ukraine is at the center of conversations between soldiers on leave and 'ordinary' passengers.

headline: 'I stared death in the face!' On board the Trans-Siberian, a 9,000-kilometer journey through an exhausted Russia
subhead: 'On Board the Trans-Siberian' (1/2). In late December 2025 and early 2026, on the train linking Moscow to Vladivostok, soldiers returning from Ukraine cross paths with cautious travelers, careful about what they say. Beyond the Urals, propaganda posters attempt by every possible means to recruit new soldiers.

headline: Thomas Piketty: '1789, 1945, 2026: Three French debt crises'
